lobbying_activities: Aoife McEvilly Coimisiún na Meán cuairt ar TG4 ar 30/5/2025, plé ar mhaoiniú ilbhliaintiúil do TG4 agus ceist Moladh 7.1 do cheannas eagarthóireacht neamhspleách ar Nuacht TG4. Chruinniú le Deirdre Ní Choistín Ard Stiúrthóir. - Meeting

Cruinniú  i TG4 ar 9/7/2025 leis an Ard Stiúrthóir Deirdre Ní Choistín, ard bainistíocht TG4 agus Cathaoirleach TG4  leis an Aire Patrick O'Donovan, Ard Rúnaí Feargal Ó Coigligh agus Rúnaí Cúnta Tríona Quill. Pléadh tosaíochtaí straitéiseacha TG4 do 2026, ach go háirithe maoiniú leordhóthanach agus ilbhliaintiúíl TG4, Neamhspleáchas nuachta agus infheicfheacht agus infhaighteacht ábhair TG4 ar ardáin 3ú páirtí. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Briefing for political representatives in Buswell's Hotel, Dublin on the biomethane sector and the policy / support requirements for the mobilisation of the sector.
records.intended_results: Lobbied TD's and Senators on the main items required to mobilise the biomethane sector in Ireland including: 1.A Government statement reaffirming its commitment to  biomethane strategy. 2.The immediate publication of the full design of the RHO scheme.3.Government  removes all eligibility in existing and new obligation schemes for fraudulent imported (Non EU produced) liquid biofuels.  4.Extends the deadline and commit to funding the 18 project under the capital grant scheme.
lobbying_activities: Lobby day

records.specific_details: Seeking Government measures to support the continued recovery of Ireland's tourism and hospitality industry
records.intended_results: Restoration of the 9% rate of VAT for hospitality food services::Measures to improve cost competitiveness and avoid any further cost increases::For a review to be carried out of options for assisting hotels to develop employer-led staff accommodation across the sector::Targeted funding for a national hotel retrofitting scheme to reduce carbon footprint::Increased investment and support for tourism marketing both domestically and overseas.::Insurance reform and policy to avoid any additional increases in insurance costs::Increased investment in training and skills development within the sector::Robust enforcement of regulations around short term lettings::Additional resources for tourism economic research::Avoid the introduction of a tourist tax::Increased air access including removal of cap at Dublin airport

records.specific_details: Liaison on the upcoming launch of the Real Face of Men's Health report which makes clear the need for the government to progress the National Men's Health Action plan, with a range of recommendations on priority areas including the need for cross government support statement and clarity on initial funding for the action plan.
records.intended_results: TDs and Ministers show their public support for action on men's health with statements in the Dáil, attendance at events and meetings with Movember and progression of the national men's health action plan as per the recommendation in the Real Face of Men's Health report available here https://ie.movember.com/movember-institute/the-real-face-of-mens-health-report
lobbying_activities: Danny has been contacting TDs and the team for the Minister of Health to arrange meetings ahead of launch and share information on the report - Email
We have met with policy makers on 1 on 1 meetings and via the Movember stand at the National Ploughing Championships 2025 - Meeting

records.specific_details: Outreach to relevant stakeholders to introduce Liquid Gas Ireland’s newly appointed Senior Director for Government Affairs and Policy, Alan Farrell, and highlight LGI’s policy priorities for rural consumers and businesses.
records.intended_results: To brief political representatives on LGI’s policy priorities to support the decarbonisation of rural homes and businesses beyond the natural gas grid, emphasising the need for a ‘Just Transition’ in Ireland and the potential impact of the upcoming Renewable Heat Obligation Bill.

records.lobbyist_name: Sligo Chamber of Commerce and Industry
records.specific_details: Provide funding for N17 Sligo to Ireland West International Airport
records.intended_results: This condition has disconnected the project from its origin as a key project to deliver Enhanced Regional Connectivity and to provide accessibility to the North West. This cannot be advanced at present due to the existing ministerial condition. It is critical that the entire project, as envisaged on launch of the National Planning Framework is delivered for the North West Region, and that the original promise of the project is not lost or diluted along the way.

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: Meeting held with both Michael Healy Rae, Minister of State DAFM,  and Frank Feighan TD at Masonite premises. This was part of a Ministerial visit to forestry related meetings & other appointments in the Sligo-Leitrim region.  The ministers were keen to visit Masonite to experience how wood residue is utilised to manufacture door panels for export. This is one of the major outputs for commercial wood products in the region.
records.intended_results: Meeting was requested by the minister to understand how wood residue is utilised to manufacture door panels for export
